description_long: This sculpture depicts a draped, headless and armless human torso, carved in a classical style reminiscent of ancient Greco-Roman statuary. The figure is clothed in flowing robes with deep, naturalistic folds that cascade down the body, suggesting movement and the weight of fabric. The surface is weathered and textured, with some areas appearing smoother from wear, while others retain more pronounced chisel marks. The base is irregular and roughly hewn, indicating it may have once been part of a larger structure or plinth. There is a visible marking or number on the front lower section, possibly an inventory or catalog number. The overall coloration is a muted, earthy beige, typical of aged stone or marble.
description: A weathered, headless and armless classical torso sculpture draped in flowing robes, carved from beige stone with visible surface texture and markings.
description_view_0: The left side of the draped torso is visible, showing deep folds in the robe and the irregular, rough base.
description_view_1: The front of the sculpture reveals the most detailed drapery and a visible marking or number on the lower section.
description_view_2: The right side view highlights the curvature of the torso and the continuation of the robe's folds.
description_view_3: The back of the sculpture is less detailed, with smoother surfaces and less pronounced drapery folds.
attribute_location_context: No specific setting is visible; the background is neutral and does not indicate an environment.
